hypothesis: An equivariant graph neural network trained on the XDIP dataset to predict Fe K-edge XANES spectra from local iron coordination geometry will achieve significantly lower prediction error than non-equivariant GNN baselines (GCN, GAT, GIN) specifically for iron centers with low-symmetry or mixed-ligand coordination environments, because explicitly encoding SE(3) symmetry into the model will allow it to distinguish stereochemically distinct configurations that produce subtly different pre-edge features—a capability testable by stratifying test-set MAE across coordination symmetry classes defined by iron point group symmetry extracted from the XDIP structural annotations.
